236	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
237	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
238	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
239	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
240		Caps evidence.Caps
241	}

249	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
250	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
251	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
252	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
253		Prompt   string
254		Overflow bool

256	}

258	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
259	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
260	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
261	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
262		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
263	}
264	
265	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
266	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
267	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
268	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
269		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
270	}
271	
272	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
273	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
274	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
275	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
276	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
277	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
278	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
279	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
280		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
281	}

284	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
285	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
286		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, true)

289	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
290		caps := opts.Caps
291		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
292			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
293		}
294	
295		var sb strings.Builder
296		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
297	
298		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
299	
300		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
301		beadStart := sb.Len()
302		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
303		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
304	
305		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
306			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
307		} else {
308			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
309		}
310	
311		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
312			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
313		} else {
314			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
315		}
316	
317		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
318			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
319		}
320	
321		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
322			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
323		}
324	
325		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
326		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
327			Name:          "bead",
328			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
329			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
330		})
331	
332		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
333		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
334		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
335			invStart := sb.Len()
336			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
337			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
338			for _, f := range allFiles {
339				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
340				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
341			}
342			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
343			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
344				Name:          "changed-files",
345				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
346				SelectedItems: paths,
347			})
348		}
349	
350		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
351		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
352		if len(refs) == 0 {
353			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
354		} else {
355			for _, ref := range refs {
356				if ref.Title != "" {
357					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
358				} else {
359					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
360				}
361				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
362				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
363				if readErr != nil {
364					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
365					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
366						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
367						TruncationReason: "read_error",
368						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
369					})
370				} else {
371					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
372					if truncated {
373						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
374					}
375					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
376					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
377						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
378						BytesIncluded: len(content),
379						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
380					}
381					if truncated {
382						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
383						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
384					}
385					sections = append(sections, sec)
386				}
387				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
388			}
389		}
390		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
391	
392		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
393		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
394		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
395		diffSection.Name = "diff"
396		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
397		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
398	
399		if includeProse {
400			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
401			if proseErr != nil {
402				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
403				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
404				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
405				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
406					Name:          "prose-review",
407					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
408				})
409			} else {
410				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
411				sb.WriteString("\n")
412				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
413			}
414		}
415	
416		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
417		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
418		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
419		if includeProse {
420			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
421		}
422		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
423	
424		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
425	
426		out := sb.String()
427		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
428			Name:    "review-prompt",
429			Content: out,
430		}}, len(out))
431		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
432			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
433			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
434			Sections: sections,
435		}
436	}

438	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
439		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
440		if err != nil {
441			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
442		}
443	
444		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
445		if err != nil {
446			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
447		}
448		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
449		if err != nil {
450			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
451		}
452	
453		var src strings.Builder
454		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
455			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
456		}
457		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
458			src.WriteString(desc)
459			src.WriteString("\n\n")
460		}
461		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
462			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
463			src.WriteString(acc)
464			src.WriteString("\n\n")
465		}
466		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
467			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
468			src.WriteString(notes)
469			src.WriteString("\n")
470		}
471	
472		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
473		var sb strings.Builder
474		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
475		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
476		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
477		if len(findings) == 0 {
478			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
479		} else {
480			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
481			for _, finding := range findings {
482				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
483				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
484				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
485				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
486			}
487			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
488		}
489		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
490	
491		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
492			Name:          "prose-review",
493			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
494		}
495		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
496		return sb.String(), section, nil
497	}

499	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
500	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
501	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
502	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
503	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
504	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
505	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
506		if len(files) == 0 {
507			return originalDiff
508		}
509		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
510			if f.Path == "" {
511				return 3
512			}
513			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
514				return 0
515			}
516			for _, r := range refs {
517				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
518					return 1
519				}
520			}
521			return 2
522		}
523		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
524		copy(ordered, files)
525		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
526	
527		var b2 strings.Builder
528		for _, f := range ordered {
529			body := f.Body
530			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
531				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
532			}
533			b2.WriteString(body)
534		}
535		return b2.String()
536	}
537	
538	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
539	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
540	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
541	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
542	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
543		var b strings.Builder
544		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
545		b.WriteString(f.Path)
546		b.WriteString(" b/")
547		b.WriteString(f.Path)
548		b.WriteString("\n")
549		if f.Stat != "" {
550			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
551			b.WriteString("\n")
552		}
553		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
554			b.WriteString(h)
555			b.WriteString("\n")
556		}
557		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
558		return b.String()
559	}

561	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
562	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
563	// exact symbolic resolution).
564	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
565		if text == "" || p == "" {
566			return false
567		}
568		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
569			return true
570		}
571		base := path.Base(p)
572		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
573	}
574	
575	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
576	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
577	// documents.
578	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
579		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
580			return false
581		}
582		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
583		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
584			return true
585		}
586		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
587			return true
588		}
589		return false
590	}

592	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
593	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
594		var buf bytes.Buffer
595		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
596			"&", "&",
597			"<", "<",
598			">", ">",
599		).Replace(s))
600		return buf.String()
601	}

603	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
604	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).

607		ProjectRoot string
608		BeadStore   BeadReader
609		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
610		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
611		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
612		Service agentlib.FizeauService
613		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
614		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
615		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
616		Runner AgentRunner
617		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
618		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
619		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.

629		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
630		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved

636	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
637	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
638	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
639	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
640	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
641	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
642	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
643	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
644		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
645	}

647	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
648	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
649	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
650	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
651	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
652	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
653		correlation := map[string]string{}
654		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
655			correlation[k] = v
656		}
657		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
658		if meta.GroupID != "" {
659			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
660			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
661		}
662		if impl.Harness != "" {
663			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
664		}
665		if impl.Provider != "" {
666			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
667		}
668		if impl.Model != "" {
669			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
670		}
671		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
672			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
673		}
674		minPower := 0
675		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
676			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
677		}
678		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
679		return AgentRunRuntime{
680			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
681			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
682			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
683			Correlation:         correlation,
684			Role:                "reviewer",
685			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
686			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
687			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
688			ClearProfile:        true,
689			ClearMaxPower:       true,
690		}
691	}

697	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) reviewerDispatchProfile {
698		floor := 0
699		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
700			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
701		}
702		if r.Runner != nil {
703			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
704		}
705		selectedSvc := r.Service
706		if selectedSvc == nil {
707			factory := serviceRunFactory
708			if factory == nil {
709				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
710			}
711			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
712			if err != nil {
713				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
714			}
715			selectedSvc = built
716		}
717		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
718		if err != nil {
719			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
720		}
721		name := ""
722		if floor > 0 {
723			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, floor)
724		} else {
725			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
726		}
727		if name == "" {
728			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
729		}
730		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
731			if profile.Name == name {
732				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: profile.MinPower}
733			}
734		}
735		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: floor}
736	}

738	func selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string {
739		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
740			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
741				return profile.Name
742			}
743		}
744		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
745	}

762	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
763		if len(correlation) == 0 {
764			return ""
765		}
766		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
767		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
768			parts = append(parts, beadID)
769		}
770		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
771			parts = append(parts, groupID)
772		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
773			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
774		}
775		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
776			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
777		}
778		if len(parts) == 0 {
779			return ""
780		}
781		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
782	}

811	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
812	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
813	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.
814	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error) {
815		reviewer := *r
816		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
817			reviewer.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
818		}
819		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
820			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
821		}
822		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
823		if beadID == "" {
824			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
825		}
826		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
827		if err != nil {
828			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
829		}
830		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
831		if workDir == "" {
832			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
833		}
834		impl := ImplementerRouting{
835			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
836			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
837			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
838			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
839			Correlation: map[string]string{
840				"bead_id":       beadID,
841				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
842				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
843				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
844				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
845				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
846			},
847		}
848		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir)
849		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
850		if review == nil {
851			if reduceErr != nil {
852				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
853			}
854			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
855		}
856		out := CandidateReviewResult{
857			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
858			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
859			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
860			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
861			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
862		}
863		if reduceErr != nil {
864			return out, reduceErr
865		}
866		return out, groupErr
867	}

869	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting, diff, reviewWorkDir string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
870		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
871		if err != nil {
872			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
873		}
874	
875		// Resolve governing document references.
876		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
877	
878		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
879		iter := 1
880	
881		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
882		caps := r.Caps
883		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
884			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
885		}
886		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
887		prompt := built.Prompt
888		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
889		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
890		if err != nil {
891			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
892		}
893		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
894			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
895		}
896	
897		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
898		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
899		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
900		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
901		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
902		if built.Overflow {
903			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
904			reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl)
905			reviewRouteLabel := reviewProfile.Name
906			if r.Model != "" {
907				reviewRouteLabel = r.Model
908			} else if r.ModelRef != "" {
909				reviewRouteLabel = r.ModelRef
910			}
911			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
912				Sections:    built.Sections,
913				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
914				OutputBytes: 0,
915				Harness:     r.Harness,
916				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
917			}
918			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
919				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
920				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
921				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
922				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
923				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
924				BaseRev:         baseRev,
925				ResultRev:       resultRev,
926				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
927				CostUSD:         0,
928				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
929				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
930			}
931			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
932				Harness:          r.Harness,
933				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
934				BaseRev:          baseRev,
935				ResultRev:        resultRev,
936				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
937				BeadID:           beadID,
938				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
939				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
940			}, reviewArtifactResult{
941				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
942				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
943				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
944			})
945			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
946				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
947		}
948	
949		// Resolve reviewer routing. An explicit reviewer harness/model wins as an
950		// operator override; otherwise DDx sends only a fizeau profile hint plus
951		// the reviewer MinPower floor.
952		reviewHarness := r.Harness
953		reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl)
954	
955		start := time.Now()
956		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewProfile.Name)
957		if runRuntime.MinPowerOverride == 0 && reviewProfile.MinPower > 0 {
958			runRuntime.MinPowerOverride = reviewProfile.MinPower
959		}
960		reviewRouteLabel := r.applyExplicitReviewerPins(&runRuntime)
961		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
962		runRuntime.WorkDir = reviewWorkDir
963		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
964	
965		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
966		if runErr != nil {
967			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
968			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
969			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
970			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
971				Sections:    built.Sections,
972				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
973				OutputBytes: 0,
974				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
975				Harness:     reviewHarness,
976				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
977			}
978			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
979				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
980				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
981				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
982				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
983				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
984				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
985				ResultRev:       resultRev,
986				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
987				DurationMS:      durationMS,
988				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
989				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
990				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
991			}
992			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
993				Harness:          reviewHarness,
994				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
995				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
996				ResultRev:        resultRev,
997				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
998				BeadID:           beadID,
999				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1000				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1001			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1002				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1003				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
1004				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1005				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1006			})
1007			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
1008		}
1009	
1010		actualHarness := reviewHarness
1011		actualModel := reviewRouteLabel
1012		actualProvider := ""
1013		actualPower := 0
1014		if result != nil {
1015			if result.Harness != "" {
1016				actualHarness = result.Harness
1017			}
1018			if result.Model != "" {
1019				actualModel = result.Model
1020			}
1021			actualProvider = result.Provider
1022			actualPower = result.ActualPower
1023			durationMS = result.DurationMS
1024		}
1025	
1026		output := ""
1027		sessionID := ""
1028		if result != nil {
1029			output = result.Output
1030			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
1031		}
1032		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
1033		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
1034		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
1035		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
1036		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
1037		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
1038		var strictVerdict Verdict
1039		var rationale string
1040		var findings []Finding
1041		if parseErr == nil {
1042			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
1043			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
1044			findings = parsed.Findings
1045			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
1046				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
1047				for _, f := range findings {
1048					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
1049					if line == "" {
1050						continue
1051					}
1052					if f.Location != "" {
1053						line = f.Location + ": " + line
1054					}
1055					parts = append(parts, line)
1056				}
1057				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1058			}
1059		}
1060		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1061		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1062			Sections:    built.Sections,
1063			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1064			OutputBytes: len(output),
1065			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1066			Harness:     actualHarness,
1067			Model:       actualModel,
1068		}
1069		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1070			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
1071			Rationale:        rationale,
1072			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1073			Findings:         findings,
1074			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1075			RawOutput:        output,
1076			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
1077			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
1078			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
1079			SessionID:        sessionID,
1080			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1081			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1082			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1083			DurationMS:       durationMS,
1084			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
1085			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
1086			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
1087		}
1088	
1089		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
1090		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
1091		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
1092		// regression. Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the
1093		// review outcome itself.
1094		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
1095			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1096				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
1097				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
1098				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
1099				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1100				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1101			})
1102		}
1103		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1104			Harness:          actualHarness,
1105			Model:            actualModel,
1106			SessionID:        sessionID,
1107			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1108			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1109			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1110			BeadID:           beadID,
1111			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1112			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1113		}, reviewArtifactResult{
1114			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1115			Rationale:        rationale,
1116			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1117			Findings:         findings,
1118			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1119			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1120			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1121		})
1122		if parseErr != nil {
1123			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
1124			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
1125			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
1126			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
1127			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
1128			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1129			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
1130				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1131			}
1132			reviewRes.Error = class
1133			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
1134		}
1135		return reviewRes, nil
1136	}
1137	
1138	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
1139	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
1140	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
1141	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
1142	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
1143	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
1144	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
1145	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
1146		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
1147		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
1148	}
1149	
1150	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
1151	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
1152	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1153	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1154	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1155		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1156		defer cancel()
1157		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1158		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1159		if err != nil {
1160			return "", err
1161		}
1162		return string(out), nil
1163	}
1164	
1165	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitDiff(baseRev, resultRev string) (string, error) {
1166		if strings.TrimSpace(baseRev) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(resultRev) == "" {
1167			return "", fmt.Errorf("base_rev and result_rev are required")
1168		}
1169		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1170		defer cancel()
1171		args := append([]string{"diff", baseRev, resultRev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1172		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1173		if err != nil {
1174			return "", err
1175		}
1176		return string(out), nil
1177	}
1178	
1179	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
1180		if resultRev == "" {
1181			return ""
1182		}
1183		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
1184		if err != nil {
1185			return ""
1186		}
1187		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1188	}
1189	
1190	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
1191		if artifacts == nil {
1192			return nil
1193		}
1194		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
1195			return err
1196		}
1197		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
1198	}
1199	
1200	func resultCost(result *Result) float64 {
1201		if result == nil {
1202			return 0
1203		}
1204		return result.CostUSD
1205	}

325	// TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage is the AST gate that prevents future drift
326	// in execute_bead_review.go: any new direct os.ReadFile or os/exec
327	// "git show" call must be marked //evidence:allow-unbounded.
328	func TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage(t *testing.T) {
329		src := "execute_bead_review.go"
330		fset := token.NewFileSet()
331		file, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, src, nil, parser.ParseComments)
332		require.NoError(t, err)
333	
334		// Import check.
335		importsEvidence := false
336		for _, imp := range file.Imports {
337			if imp.Path != nil && imp.Path.Value == `"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"` {
338				importsEvidence = true
339			}
340		}
341		assert.True(t, importsEvidence,
342			"execute_bead_review.go must import cli/internal/evidence (FEAT-022)")
343	
344		// Walk the AST. For every os.ReadFile or osexec.Command("git","show",...)
345		// call, climb to the enclosing statement and look for an adjacent
346		// //evidence:allow-unbounded directive in the file's comment map.
347		cmap := ast.NewCommentMap(fset, file, file.Comments)
348	
349		hasAllow := func(node ast.Node) bool {
350			// Walk parents via ast.Inspect — simplest is to scan all comment
351			// groups whose end position is just before the node's start, on a
352			// preceding line.
353			nodePos := fset.Position(node.Pos())
354			for _, cg := range file.Comments {
355				cgEnd := fset.Position(cg.End())
356				if cgEnd.Filename != nodePos.Filename {
357					continue
358				}
359				if cgEnd.Line >= nodePos.Line-3 && cgEnd.Line < nodePos.Line {
360					for _, c := range cg.List {
361						if strings.Contains(c.Text, "evidence:allow-unbounded") {
362							return true
363						}
364					}
365				}
366			}
367			// Also check the comment map: if any associated comment carries the
368			// directive on this node, treat as allowed.
369			for n, groups := range cmap {
370				if n.Pos() <= node.Pos() && node.End() <= n.End() {
371					for _, cg := range groups {
372						for _, c := range cg.List {
373							if strings.Contains(c.Text, "evidence:allow-unbounded") {
374								return true
375							}
376						}
377					}
378				}
379			}
380			return false
381		}
382	
383		var violations []string
384		ast.Inspect(file, func(n ast.Node) bool {
385			call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr)
386			if !ok {
387				return true
388			}
389			sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
390			if !ok {
391				return true
392			}
393			ident, ok := sel.X.(*ast.Ident)
394			if !ok {
395				return true
396			}
397			// os.ReadFile
398			if ident.Name == "os" && sel.Sel.Name == "ReadFile" {
399				if !hasAllow(call) {
400					p := fset.Position(call.Pos())
401					violations = append(violations, "os.ReadFile at line "+evidItoa(p.Line))
402				}
403				return true
404			}
405			// osexec.Command("git","show",...)
406			if (ident.Name == "osexec" || ident.Name == "exec") && sel.Sel.Name == "Command" {
407				if len(call.Args) >= 2 {
408					if isStringLit(call.Args[0], "git") && isStringLit(call.Args[1], "show") {
409						if !hasAllow(call) {
410							p := fset.Position(call.Pos())
411							violations = append(violations, "exec.Command(\"git\",\"show\",...) at line "+evidItoa(p.Line))
412						}
413					}
414				}
415			}
416			return true
417		})
418	
419		assert.Empty(t, violations,
420			"execute_bead_review.go must route file reads and git show through cli/internal/evidence (or annotate with //evidence:allow-unbounded)")
421	}
422	
423	// countingRunner is an AgentRunner stub that increments calls on every
424	// Run invocation, used to assert the pre-dispatch short-circuit avoids
425	// the provider call entirely.
426	type countingRunner struct {
427		calls  int
428		result *Result
429		err    error
430	}
431	
432	func (c *countingRunner) Run(_ RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
433		c.calls++
434		return c.result, c.err
435	}
436	
437	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
438	// helpers
439	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
440	
441	func filepathSafe(i int) string {
442		return "pkg/file_" + evidItoa(i) + ".go"
443	}
444	
445	func evidItoa(i int) string {
446		if i == 0 {
447			return "0"
448		}
449		neg := false
450		if i < 0 {
451			neg = true
452			i = -i
453		}
454		var buf [20]byte
455		pos := len(buf)
456		for i > 0 {
457			pos--
458			buf[pos] = byte('0' + i%10)
459			i /= 10
460		}
461		out := string(buf[pos:])
462		if neg {
463			return "-" + out
464		}
465		return out
466	}
467	
468	func isStringLit(e ast.Expr, want string) bool {
469		bl, ok := e.(*ast.BasicLit)
470		if !ok || bl.Kind != token.STRING {
471			return false
472		}
473		return bl.Value == "\""+want+"\""
474	}
